jobs.description: GreenMealCard — WordPress Website Plan General Design Direction The site should feel organic, premium and fresh — think dark forest green (#1A3A2A) as the primary brand color, with soft mint greens and warm cream backgrounds. Typography should mix a serif display font (like Playfair Display) for headlines with a clean sans-serif (like DM Sans) for body text. No generic stock-photo vibes — the aesthetic should feel like a high-end food brand meets a local farmers market. Pages to Build 1. Homepage (Main Landing Page) This is the most important page. It should contain the following sections in order: Sticky Navigation Bar — dark green background, logo "greenmealcard" on the left (with "meal" in a brighter green accent), and nav links on the right: Subscriptions, Choose Local, For Businesses, and a CTA button "Sign Up" in bright green with rounded corners. Hero Section — full-width dark green gradient background. Contains a small pill-shaped badge reading "Fresh · Local · Sustainable", a large serif headline like "Every day a fresh meal, chosen by you", a short subline about local ingredients and flexibility, and two buttons: a primary "Choose your subscription" and a ghost/outline "How does it work?". Subscription Plans Section — three side-by-side cards on a cream background. Each card shows an icon, plan name, short description, price per meal, a list of included features, and a call-to-action button. The middle card (Premium) should be visually highlighted with a darker border and a "Most popular" badge at the top. Plans are: Basic at €5/meal, Premium at €10/meal, Chef's Kitchen at €15/meal. Green Meal Savings Strip — full-width dark green band. Headline "Save Green Meals", short explanation that after every 100 meals you receive one free meal. Show a visual row of 100 small circles/dots, with the first ones filled in green (to show progress) and the 100th dot highlighted in gold as the free meal reward. How It Works Section — soft green background, four steps in a horizontal row: 1. Choose your subscription, 2. Select your local kitchen, 3. Fresh preparation, 4. Save & enjoy. Each step has a numbered circle, a short title and two sentences of explanation. Local Partners Section — cream background. Grid of partner cards, each showing an emoji or food icon, business name, cuisine type, and a star rating. The last card in the grid should be a dashed-border "Is this your business? Join us →" teaser card. Savings Calculator — a white card with two sliders: one for meals per week, one for subscription level. Below the sliders, three calculated results update live: weekly cost, how often a free meal is earned, and yearly savings amount. Business Partner Section — light green gradient background, centered text, two side-by-side info cards showing the one-time €500 joining fee and the 20% revenue share model, with a "Register as partner" button below. Footer — dark green, centered logo, links to Privacy, Terms, Contact, and a small copyright line. 2. Subscriptions Page A dedicated page that goes deeper into each plan. Show a full comparison table with all features listed per plan. Include a short FAQ section below addressing questions like "Can I switch plans?", "What if I skip a week?", and "How does the savings system work exactly?". End with a CTA to sign up. 3. Local Kitchens Page A map view (Google Maps embed or similar) showing all connected local businesses in the user's area, plus a filterable card grid below. Filter options include cuisine type, rating, distance, and dietary preferences (vegan, vegetarian, gluten-free). Each business card links to a full partner profile page showing their story, photos, menu, and reviews. 4. Green Meal Savings Page Explains the loyalty program in full detail. Visual timeline showing the journey from meal 1 to meal 100. A personal progress tracker (requires login) showing how many meals the user has ordered and how close they are to their next free meal. FAQ about how points are counted, whether they expire, and how the free meal is redeemed. 5. For Businesses Page Targeted at restaurant owners, butchers, caterers and local food businesses who want to join. Should include: a clear value proposition headline, a two-column layout showing the cost structure (€500 one-time + 20% revenue share), a list of benefits (new customer reach, no fixed monthly costs, ratings and reviews, dashboard access), and a contact/application form. Optionally include two or three testimonial quotes from existing partner businesses. 6. My GreenMealCard (User Dashboard — behind login) Personal area where logged-in users can: view their active subscription and meal history, see their savings progress (X out of 100 meals toward next free meal), manage their selected local kitchens, update dietary preferences, and manage billing. This requires a membership plugin in WordPress (like MemberPress or Paid Memberships Pro) combined with Stripe for payments. 7. Contact & FAQ Page Standard page with an accordion-style FAQ section covering the most common questions for both consumers and business partners. Below the FAQ, a simple contact form (name, email, subject, message) and optionally a live chat widget. WordPress-Specific Setup Recommendations Theme: Use a block-based theme like Kadence, GeneratePress or Blocksy — these give the most design flexibility without heavy page builder bloat. Alternatively use Elementor Pro for pixel-perfect control matching the design. Key Plugins to install: Elementor Pro or Kadence Blocks — for custom page layouts WooCommerce or MemberPress — for subscription management and payments Stripe for WooCommerce — for payment processing WPForms or Gravity Forms — for contact and partner application forms WPML or TranslatePress — if you want Dutch/English toggle Yoast SEO — for search engine optimization WP Rocket — for performance and caching Slider Revolution or MetaSlider — if you want animated hero sections Custom Post Types needed: Partners (each local kitchen as its own post with: name, cuisine type, logo, photos, description, rating) Meal Plans (to manage and display the three subscription tiers) Reviews (connected to partner posts) Color palette to configure in WordPress: Primary dark green: #1A3A2A Medium green: #2D6A4F Accent green: #52B788 Light green: #B7E4C7 Background cream: #FAF8F3 Warm mint: #E8F4ED Fonts to add via Google Fonts: Playfair Display (headings and hero text) DM Sans (body text, buttons, labels) Content Needed Before Launch Before the site goes live, you will need to prepare the following content: a professional logo file in SVG format, photos or illustrations for each subscription plan, photos of at least 5–6 local partner businesses (with permission), one clear hero image or illustration for the homepage, short copy texts for every section in both Dutch and English if you plan to serve both audiences, and a legal privacy policy and terms & conditions page.

jobs.description: We are an ecommerce fitness equipment company looking for a Google Workspace and Monday.com automation specialist to audit, troubleshoot, fix, and document our email-to-Monday routing workflows. We use Gmail, Google Workspace, Google Groups, aliases, forwarding rules, Monday.com boards, Shopify, BigCommerce, Amazon order notification emails, and automation tools such as n8n, Make.com, Zapier, APIs, or webhooks where needed. We have several broken or unreliable workflows where emails should create Monday.com tasks or route to the correct operational board. This affects orders, shipping, customer support, service, sourcing, and internal task routing. This is a takeover/audit/fix project. We do not want someone to rebuild everything from scratch before mapping the current setup. Current issues may include: * Shopify / BigCommerce / Amazon order emails not creating items on the correct Monday.com board. * Google Workspace forwarding rules or Google Groups not routing to the correct Monday.com board addresses. * External emails needing to create tasks while internal chatter should not create unnecessary tasks. * Some shared inboxes creating tasks on the wrong personal or department board. * Monday.com Secure/external board addresses vs * Custom/internal board addresses needing to be used correctly. * Multi-recipient external emails needing testing to see if Google can create isolated one-to-one forwarded copies. * If Google forwarding is not reliable, we may need a controlled fallback using n8n, Make.com, Zapier, Monday API, or another approved workflow tool. You will be responsible for: * Auditing Google Workspace Admin, Gmail forwarding, aliases, Google Groups, routing rules, and related inbox settings. Auditing Monday.com board email addresses and confirming which addresses are safe for internal vs external senders. * Mapping current routing by department / board / inbox / sender type. * Testing internal sender vs external sender behavior. * Testing one-email-to-multiple-board workflows and documenting whether Google forwarding can reliably create separate isolated copies. * Fixing the highest-priority broken workflows first, especially order notification routing. * Recommending API/middleware fallback only when forwarding is not reliable. * Documenting every change made. * Creating a written runbook and Loom walkthrough so our internal team can understand and maintain the setup. You must work carefully. Do not turn on old automations, old Make/Zapier/n8n scenarios, new apps, or new tools without approval. Do not make broad production changes before mapping the current setup. Deliverables: * Audit report of current Google Workspace / Gmail / Google Groups / Monday.com routing. * Routing map showing inboxes, aliases, groups, Monday boards, and board email addresses. * Priority fix list. * Test results for internal vs external sender behavior. * Test results for multi-board routing. * Fixed priority routing issues. * Recommendation for Google forwarding vs n8n/Make/Zapier/Monday API fallback. * Written runbook. * Loom walkthrough. * Final maintenance checklist. Must-have experience: * Google Workspace Admin * Gmail routing / aliases / forwarding * Google Groups * Monday.com board email addresses and automations * Monday.com workflow troubleshooting * n8n, Make.com, Zapier, APIs, or webhooks * Shopify / ecommerce order notification workflows * Debugging inherited automations * Clear documentation and Loom walkthroughs Please do not apply if you only have general VA experience or basic Monday.com board usage. We need someone who can troubleshoot routing logic, email behavior, and automation edge cases. Pacific Time overlap is preferred because some issues affect live operations.
jobs.title: Google Workspace / Gmail / Monday.com Routing Automation

jobs.description: Project description: Online Outfitters Canada is a Winnipeg-based hunting, fishing, and outdoor retailer running on Shopify with the Pursuit theme. The theme has accumulated a few bugs and Google indexing issues that need to be cleaned up. Looking for a Shopify-experienced developer to address everything in a single engagement, working on a duplicated theme (not the live theme), with changes pushed to live only after I review and approve. Tasks: Subscription/deferred purchase text leak. Product pages display the following text even though no products use Shopify subscriptions, deferred purchase, or pre-order: "This item is a deferred, subscription, or recurring purchase. By continuing, I agree to the cancellation policy and authorize you to charge my payment method at the prices, frequency and dates listed on this page until my order is fulfilled or I cancel, if permitted." Find where this is being rendered (likely a Liquid conditional in main-product.liquid, product-form.liquid, or a buy-buttons snippet, or possibly injected by an installed app) and ensure it only displays for products with actual selling_plan_groups configured. You can verify the bug live on: https://onlineoutfitters.ca/products/online-outfitters-fishing-backpack https://onlineoutfitters.ca/products/online-outfitters-see-through-hunting-blind Generic Shopify chrome leaking into Google's index. Phrases like "Quick view," "Choosing a selection results in a full page refresh," and "Use left/right arrows to navigate the slideshow" are being indexed by Google as page content. Add aria-hidden="true" where appropriate or move into JavaScript-rendered content so search engines don't see them. Empty SKU field on Online Outfitters Fishing Backpack product page. The page displays an empty SKU label. Either populate the SKU in Shopify admin or hide the SKU display block when no SKU is set. Verify subscription text fix on these specific URLs after fixing: https://onlineoutfitters.ca/products/online-outfitters-fishing-backpack https://onlineoutfitters.ca/products/online-outfitters-see-through-hunting-blind Plus 3-5 other product pages of your choice across different product types to confirm the fix works site-wide Working approach: Duplicate the live Pursuit theme to a working copy named "Pursuit - Working - [your name]" Make all changes on the duplicate, not the live theme Provide preview links to the duplicate after each task is complete Do not publish to live until I review and approve Document each change in a brief summary so I know what was edited Required experience: Demonstrable Shopify theme experience (please share 2-3 stores you've worked on) Working knowledge of Liquid, Shopify schema, and basic SEO considerations Familiarity with the Dawn family of themes (Pursuit is in this family) Estimated scope: 2-4 hours of work Budget: $150-400 CAD, fixed price preferred Turnaround: 5-7 business days from engagement To apply, please send: Your hourly rate or fixed-price quote for this scope Your turnaround estimate 2-3 prior Shopify projects with links if possible Confirmation you understand changes go to a duplicated theme first
jobs.title: Shopify Pursuit Theme Bug Fixes — Small Fixed-Price Project

jobs.description: About Lemira Estee Lemira Estee is a faith-inspired beauty brand focused on emotional connection, identity, and meaning not just products. We create lipstick collections that honor timeless stories and empower modern women. Our customers are drawn to purpose, not trends. Many first experience the brand at live events, where they feel a strong emotional connection before purchasing. Our goal online is to recreate that same feeling: connection before product meaning before decision simplicity over overwhelm We are not a typical cosmetics brand. We want the website to feel: personal calm meaningful guided (not sales-driven) We often guide customers toward curated kits rather than overwhelming them with too many individual choices. Products for Women. A Movement for Everyone Who Honors Them. Here is our brand message for tone and inspiration: Lemira Estee exists to illuminate the world with beauty,faith, purpose, . We honor the sacred stories that came before us while empowering the women of today. Through thoughtful design, intentional storytelling, and refined formulations, we express a modern reflection of timeless strength, dignity, and light. Every product is created for women to be felt, to be worn with meaning, and to reflect who they truly are. Because beauty is not just seen. It is recognized. And every woman deserves to feel that recognition. I put together some questions : Our brand is built on emotional connection and meaning, not just product. How would you design a Shopify experience where a woman feels seen and connected before she makes a purchase? We sell successfully in live events where women connect emotionally with the brand. How would you recreate that same feeling on our website? Our brand includes spiritual and faith-based elements but needs to remain welcoming and inclusive. How would you balance that in messaging and design? What would you prioritize on the homepage to create both emotional connection and conversion? We offer multiple lipstick options, but many of our customers prefer curated kits. How would you guide customers toward an easy decision without overwhelming them? What should a customer feel or understand within the first 10 seconds of landing on our site? Can you share 2 Shopify stores where you improved conversion, and explain exactly what you changed and why? Do you typically help shape messaging and storytelling, or do you work from what the client provides?

jobs.description: I’m looking for an experienced Shopify developer (or team) to build a custom website for a picture framing business. The current site is built on WooCommerce, and the goal is to recreate and improve it on Shopify with a cleaner UX, better performance, and a scalable structure. This is not a basic Shopify theme customization — the site requires advanced custom functionality, including a product configurator, dynamic pricing, and customer-specific logic. Core Functionality - Customer uploads their own image - Interactive frame selection (styles, materials, etc.) - Additional options: matting, glazing, hanging hardware, etc. - Real-time preview showing the selected frame around the uploaded image - The preview should scale proportionally based on selected dimensions (real-life sizing behavior) - Dynamic pricing that updates based on size, frame, and selected options - Clean, simple, and highly intuitive user experience Customer Features Ability for customers to: - View previous orders - Reorder or edit previous designs - Edit products directly from cart - Save designs for later use Wholesale / Customer-Specific Logic - Ability to assign wholesale status to specific customers - Wholesale customers should: - See different pricing - Have minimum order quantity (MOQ) rules or adjusted pricing below MOQ - Have access to specific pages/products only (restricted visibility) Backend / Scalability Requirements - Easy-to-use backend where I can: - Add/edit frame designs - Adjust pricing rules - Add new products and pages without needing a developer - Site should be built in a scalable, modular way so future updates are simple Pages & Features Needed - Home page - Shop All Frames page - Create Custom Frame page (main configurator) - Metal Prints page - Acrylic Prints page - Canvas Prints page - Checkout (clean and optimized) - Shipping integration (ShipStation) - Customer reviews - Strong security best practices - SEO-friendly setup (apps/tools/configuration) Open to suggestions on additional features that improve conversion and usability. What I’m Looking For - Strong experience with custom Shopify development - Experience building product configurators / dynamic pricing systems - Experience with customer segmentation (wholesale vs retail logic) - Ability to recreate complex functionality and improve it - Focus on clean UI/UX and smooth flow - Efficient execution — I want this done properly without unnecessary delays Project Approach - I will share the current website with shortlisted candidates - Open to recommendations (apps vs custom vs hybrid approach) - Looking for someone who can guide decisions, not just execute To Apply Please include: 1) Examples of similar Shopify projects (configurators, pricing engines, or wholesale setups) 2) How you would approach: - Image upload + real-time scaling preview - Wholesale pricing & restricted access logic 3) Recommended setup (apps, custom code, etc.) 4) Estimated timeline Additional Notes - This is a one-time project (no long-term engagement) - Clear communication and reliability are important - Ready to start immediately Screening Question (Required) Briefly explain how you would handle: 1) A real-time image preview that scales proportionally based on selected dimensions 2) Customer-specific pricing and restricted content (wholesale vs retail) within Shopify

jobs.description: I am an academic researcher revising a manuscript on marketing analytics and real-time intent-based segmentation for a peer-reviewed journal. The peer reviewers have requested real-world validation of my proposed model. I am looking to hire a business owner, CRM manager, or marketing agency director with an active mid-sized e-commerce, SaaS, or lead-generation audience. I will pay you for your time and for allowing me to run a controlled, 14- to 30-day A/B test on a segment of your audience to compare standard trigger-based marketing against my intent-based framework. The Goal: The sole purpose of this project is to generate an anonymized, experimental dataset to prove the causal effectiveness and "trust costs" of different targeting policies in a real-world setting. What is Required: A Live Environment: You must have an active email or SMS marketing setup (e.g., Klaviyo, Braze, ActiveCampaign, etc.) tied to an e-commerce or lead-gen website. Implementation: Willingness to work with me to deploy a split test. Control Group: Your standard baseline (e.g., a standard cart abandonment or browse abandonment trigger). Test Group: My "Liquid Audience" policy, which factors in frequency caps, intent decay, and "do-nothing" rules. Data Export (Strictly NO PII): At the end of the test, I need a data export of the logs. I do not want names, emails, phone numbers, or IP addresses. I only need: Randomly hashed/anonymized User IDs. Timestamps of events (views, cart adds, messages sent). Positive outcomes (conversion rates, clicks, purchases). Negative "trust" outcomes (unsubscribes, spam complaints, opt-outs). Ideal Candidate: You own or manage an e-commerce brand, newsletter, or digital product with at least 10,000+ active subscribers/users. You have the legal authority to run marketing tests on this list. You are interested in optimizing your own conversion rates (you get to keep the optimized algorithm and any extra revenue generated during the test for free). You are comfortable exporting raw event logs and scrubbing them of PII before handing them over. Privacy & Legal: This is entirely strictly compliant with GDPR/CCPA. I only want aggregate behavioral logic and hashed rows. Your brand name can remain completely anonymous in the published research paper (e.g., "A mid-sized European cosmetics retailer"). Budget & Application: My budget is flexible depending on the size of your audience and the effort required to set up the test. Please reply with: A brief description of your niche/industry. Your approximate list size and weekly messaging volume. The marketing tech stack you currently use (e.g., Shopify + Klaviyo).

Billing: Approve vendor and freight forwarder invoices only after reconciliation is clean. FBA inbound: Manage FBA shipments end-to-end and handle claims when things go wrong. Documentation and reporting: Maintain per-vendor logistics records and submit a weekly shipment summary. Who we're looking for - 3+ years of hands-on supply chain or logistics coordination experience in ecommerce, including Amazon FBA - Strong attention to detail — reconciliation work is unforgiving; we need someone who catches the off-by-one before it becomes a problem - Highly organized — you'll be juggling many shipments at different stages at once (some being placed, some in production, some in transit, some being reconciled). jobs.description: KOOSHOO | Shopify Developer Technical Brief Ideally, we'd like a complete project cost as part of your proposal. Context We are currently working through an SEO recovery programme for KOOSHOO. Our audit has identified a set of technical issues that require Shopify developer-level access to review and resolve. Some of these may be tied to theme code, so we are not assuming all items are fully fixable - we need your expert assessment first. What we need from you: 1. Review each issue listed below 2. Advise on what is and isn't resolvable within Shopify 3. Provide a time estimate and quote for the fixes you can action 4. Fixes to proceed only once signed off by the client (Jesse Schiller, KOOSHOO) Issues to Address 1. hreflang Conflicts We have identified 88 hreflang conflicts within the page source code, plus 2 incorrect hreflang implementations. These are primarily on wholesale.kooshoo.com. The issue type is "no self-referencing hreflang" - pages are missing the required self-referencing annotation, which can cause search engines to serve incorrect regional versions to users. Data: View hreflang tab 2. Unminified JavaScript and CSS Files 320 unminified JS and CSS files have been identified, largely across wholesale.kooshoo.com pages. Unminified files increase page load times, which directly affects both user experience and Google's Core Web Vitals scoring. Data: View unminified JS/CSS tab 3. Site Speed Optimisation kooshoo.com is currently scoring poorly on both mobile and desktop according to Google's PageSpeed Insights. Google has flagged specific optimisations. Improving speed directly affects rankings and on-site conversion. ● Mobile report: https://pagespeed.web.dev/analysis/https-kooshoo-com/2m72op1qwz?form_factor=mobil e ● Desktop report: https://pagespeed.web.dev/analysis/https-kooshoo-com/2m72op1qwz?form_factor=deskt op Data: View site speed tab 4. iFrame Audit and Removal iFrames have been detected on the site. They can complicate mobile navigation and are harder for search engines to index. No specific file is associated with this issue - the developer will need to locate them via page source (search for "iframe") and assess which can be removed or replaced. Data: View iFrame tab 5. W3C Validation Errors 113 W3C errors and 14 warnings have been identified. Resolving these improves browser compatibility and adherence to web standards. W3C validator: https://validator.w3.org/nu/?doc=https%3A%2F%2Fkooshoo.com%2F Data: View W3C tab 6. 4XX System URL One 4XX URL has been flagged as a system-level URL that requires developer review: https://kooshoo.com/web-pixels-manager@0.0.221/sandbox/collections/org anic-cotton-plastic-free-scrunchies This appears to be a Shopify system URL. We need confirmation on whether this can be redirected or should be excluded from SEO crawl scope. Data: View 4XX tab Important Notes We understand some of these issues may be constrained by Shopify's theme architecture and not fully resolvable. The hreflang and unminified file issues are predominantly on wholesale.kooshoo.com, which is treated as a separate entity from the main site - please flag if the fix approach differs between the two. No work should proceed until a quote has been reviewed and approved by the client. Next Steps 1. Developer reviews all six issues and the linked data 2. Developer provides a triage summary: what's fixable, what isn't, and why 3. Developer provides time estimate and quote 4. Abi (Team Empathy) presents quote to Jesse for approval 5. Work commences on confirmed items

jobs.description: HARA & HINU - Customer Service Representative (Part-Time, Remote) WHO ARE WE? HARA The Label is a Melbourne-based fashion label creating elevated, everyday underwear and clothing essentials designed for the modern woman. Our collections are thoughtfully crafted with quality bamboo fabrics. We believe in building a wardrobe you love with pieces that feel as good as they look. HINU is our sister brand, offering a curated range of hair growth products and hair accessories designed with the same commitment to quality and intentional living. We’re a small but mighty team, and we’re looking for people who are passionate about delivering exceptional customer experiences to grow with us. At the heart of everything we do are our values: Quality First: We stand behind our products and take pride in every detail — from fabric to fit to customer experience. Customer Obsessed: Our customers are at the centre of every decision. We go above and beyond to make them feel valued. Integrity: We’re transparent, honest, and always aim to do the right thing. Growth Mindset: We’re always learning, improving, and looking for better ways to do things. WHAT DOES THE ROLE INVOLVE? We’re looking for a friendly, reliable, and detail-oriented Customer Service Representative to be the front line of our customer experience across both HARA and HINU brands. This is a part-time, fully remote role ideal for someone based offshore who thrives in e-commerce and loves helping people. You’ll be the first point of contact for our customers, answering questions, resolving issues, and making sure every interaction leaves a positive impression. You’ll work across email and social media channels using Gorgias, with Shopify as our e-commerce platform. KEY RESPONSIBILITIES Customer Enquiries & Support Manage and respond to customer service tickets across email, live chat, Instagram, Facebook, and TikTok in a timely and professional manner Provide accurate product information for pre-purchase enquiries including sizing guidance (HARA), materials, care instructions, and general product details (both brands) Deliver warm, on-brand communication that reflects each brand’s tone of voice Escalate complex or sensitive issues to the relevant team member with clear context Order Management & Post-Purchase Support Process and advise on returns, exchanges, and refunds in line with brand policies Investigate and resolve lost-in-transit, delayed, and damaged shipment enquiries with carriers and fulfillment partners Assist customers with order modifications, tracking updates, and delivery queries Flag recurring fulfilment or shipping issues to the operations team for resolution Systems & Process Improvement Work within Gorgias (helpdesk) and Shopify (e-commerce platform) daily Contribute to improving macros, templates, and automated flows in Gorgias to increase efficiency Help maintain and update the customer service knowledge base and FAQs Identify trends in customer feedback and flag opportunities for improvement to the team WHAT KIND OF PERSON ARE WE LOOKING FOR? You’ll be a great fit if you… Have 1–3 years of experience in e-commerce customer service (fashion or lifestyle brands preferred) Are experienced with Gorgias (or similar helpdesk platforms such as Zendesk or Freshdesk) Are familiar with Shopify and comfortable navigating orders, customer profiles, and product information Have excellent written English with a friendly, professional, and empathetic tone Are comfortable managing enquiries across multiple channels including email, live chat, and social media DMs Are highly organised, detail-oriented, and able to manage multiple tickets and priorities simultaneously Are a self-starter who can work independently in a remote environment with minimal supervision Understand the importance of brand voice and can adapt communication style across two distinct brands Are proactive in identifying process improvements and customer experience enhancements Have a genuine interest in fashion, lifestyle products, and e-commerce HOURS & DETAILS Part-time: approximately 25–30 hours per week (flexible schedule to be confirmed) Fully remote — offshore position Potential to grow into a full-time role as business volumes increase This role will be engaged via Upwork WHAT WE CAN OFFER? You’ll be joining a growing Australian brand at an exciting stage. We value our team and believe in creating an environment where everyone can do their best work. Flexible, remote working arrangement Opportunity to grow with two exciting brands Supportive, collaborative team culture Direct exposure to all areas of a fast-growing e-commerce business Product discounts across both brands HOW TO APPLY Please submit your application via Upwork with a brief cover letter outlining your relevant experience and why you’d be a great fit for this role. We’d love to hear about your experience with Gorgias and Shopify, and what great customer service means to you.

Please note: This is NOT a basic GA4 installation task. Our GA4 purchase tracking is already largely functioning correctly, including revenue and shipping values. The remaining issue is specifically around checkout funnel continuity and enhanced ecommerce event tracking. Current Situation: • Shopify store with GA4 setup already in place • Purchase/revenue tracking appears to be working • Shipping costs are now tracking correctly • Transactions are appearing in GA4 • Microsoft Clarity is installed and working • Some checkout journeys are tracking correctly • However, checkout funnel steps remain inconsistent or incomplete The Main Problem: We are seeing inconsistencies between: • begin_checkout • add_shipping_info • add_payment_info • purchase Some purchases complete correctly in GA4, but many checkout journeys are not maintaining continuity through the funnel. Example: • Shopify may show 10–12 orders • GA4 may only connect 2 of those fully through the funnel journey • Purchase events exist, but checkout attribution and step tracking remain incomplete We need someone experienced specifically in: • Shopify enhanced ecommerce tracking • Google Tag Manager (GTM) • GA4 ecommerce debugging • Shopify checkout attribution • Funnel event continuity troubleshooting • Purchase journey diagnostics We are NOT looking for: • Basic GA4 setup • Generic Shopify developers • Someone learning on the job Please only apply if you have: • Proven experience repairing Shopify GA4 funnel/event issues • Strong understanding of ecommerce attribution • Experience debugging checkout event loss/inconsistency • Previous examples/screenshots/case studies preferred Scope Required: • Audit current setup • Identify why funnel continuity is breaking • Repair or advise on repair • Validate checkout event flow • Ensure enhanced ecommerce events are correctly passing through checkout • Confirm data consistency between Shopify and GA4 Please explain: 1. Your experience with Shopify funnel tracking issues 2. What you believe commonly causes this issue 3. Your proposed approach to diagnosing and fixing it 4. Whether you would expect this to be a GA4, GTM, Shopify checkout, or attribution issue We are looking for someone who can confidently diagnose and resolve the issue rather than trial-and-error troubleshooting. Thanks Linda

jobs.description: We’re looking for a experienced Meta Ads specialist to take over and improve the paid social account for MISS SWIG, a first-to-market powdered cocktail and mocktail mixer brand. MISS SWIG is a new but proven product. We have strong customer feedback, hospitality/B2B traction, and a clear opportunity to grow D2C. We launched online in December and are now ready to build our paid acquisition properly. Our current Meta spend is around $100/day, so this is not a large account yet. We are looking for someone who knows how to work with smaller budgets, test intelligently, and build the right foundations for growth. Right now, we ship to Australia only but we will be looking to expand towards the end of the year. Website: www.missswig.com What we are looking for We need someone who can do more than launch campaigns and check results occasionally. We are looking for a Meta Ads expert who can: * Audit our current account setup and identify what needs to change * Review campaign structure, tracking, audiences, creative, copy and reporting * Rebuild or restructure campaigns where needed * Set up a clear creative testing framework * Test different hooks, angles, offers, formats and copy * Advise on cold vs warm audience strategy * Help us understand what is genuinely working and what is not * Actively review, optimise and explain performance * Make practical recommendations based on our budget, data and stage of growth We want someone who can bring proper direction to the account, not just keep spending money on a setup that may not be right. Current situation We are currently working with an agency, but are looking to move on. Some of the issues we are seeing include: * Limited creative variation being tested * The same creative being used across warm and cold audiences * Not enough testing of different hooks, copy, offers and angles * Campaign changes that do not seem to be based on the best-performing assets * Inconsistent performance, with some days producing multiple purchases in a short period and other days producing none * A lack of clear explanation around what is being tested, what is being learned, and what should happen next I have also been following wider discussions in Meta advertising communities around delivery volatility, outages, “bad delivery days” and targeting instability. Some of the timing appears to line up exactly with what we have seen in our own account. That said, we are not looking for someone who simply blames Meta for everything. We need someone who understands the current Meta landscape, but can also separate platform volatility from poor structure, weak creative, missed testing opportunities or incorrect optimisation. About the product MISS SWIG makes premium cocktail and mocktail mixers that turn into bar-quality drinks in around 10 seconds. Customers just add water and their choice of spirit, or make the same drink alcohol-free. No shaker, syrups, fresh citrus or complicated ingredients needed. The product is genuinely good, but because it is a new format, the ads need to educate quickly and overcome scepticism. Once people try it, they understand it. The challenge is helping cold audiences understand the value quickly enough to purchase. Our current offer includes a Buy 2, Get 1 Free bundle, and we want to make sure this is communicated clearly across ads, product pages and retargeting. Creative strategy and ad development We have a growing library of UGC, product imagery, brand assets and existing content. However, creative strategy and ad development are not our area of expertise. We need someone who can guide what we should actually be making and testing. We would like support with: * Identifying the strongest hooks and angles to test * Turning existing UGC into effective ads * Advising what static ads should be created * Testing product demos, testimonials, offer-led ads, founder-style content, carousels and other relevant formats * Creating or briefing new ad concepts * Helping communicate the product quickly and clearly, especially to cold audiences * If you believe the account needs stronger static creative, we need you to either be able to create these yourself or coordinate someone reliable who can. Any creative produced for the brand needs to look polished, realistic and premium. If AI tools are used at any point in the creative process, the final output needs to be excellent. We do not want ads with obvious AI errors, distorted packaging, fake-looking product labels, strange text, unrealistic hands or anything that makes the brand feel cheap or untrustworthy. Platforms and context We use Shopify and also have Instant.one, which is meant to support audience identification. We would like someone who can understand how that may or may not fit into the Meta strategy. We also recently received an email from Meta saying our account may be included in a beta for a more streamlined Sales campaign setup, including changes around catalogue connection and campaign workflow. We need someone who is across Meta updates and can advise us properly as the platform changes. We are also working separately on website conversion optimisation, particularly around improving product page clarity and making our current volume offer more compelling. The right person will have * Strong Meta Ads experience for eCommerce brands * Experience with Shopify stores * Experience working with smaller budgets and early-stage brands * A clear understanding of creative testing, not just media buying * Strong commercial thinking * Awareness of recent Meta performance volatility and platform changes * The ability to explain results and recommendations in plain English * Provide all ad materials and copy for approval before posting * A proactive approach to testing and optimisation * The ability to work with existing assets and advise what else is needed * A realistic approach to scaling from a smaller daily budget * The ability to create or source high-quality static ad creative where needed Please include in your application Please do not send a generic proposal. I will be looking closely at whether you have actually read this and understand the situation. In your reply, please include: 1. Your relevant Meta Ads experience, especially with eCommerce brands 2. Examples of brands or accounts you have worked on, ideally with similar budgets or early-stage scaling 3. How you would approach the first 2–4 weeks in an account like ours 4. Whether you are across recent Meta delivery volatility, outages or platform changes, and how you account for that in your strategy 5. Any quick thoughts after looking at www.missswig.com 6. What you think this would require in terms of time, cost and involvement 7. Whether you can develop ads yourself or have someone you recommend for this We are looking for someone thoughtful, practical and actively involved. We do not want someone who simply launches broad campaigns, uses the same creative everywhere and waits. We want to build the account properly, understand what is happening, and scale with more confidence. Open to setup fee + weekly payments / retainer. Don't ask our budget, tell us your rate.. Likely to be a long term arrangement but will depend everything being a good fit :)

Section 2: Food Section. a selection and examples of foods which are good with our chili oil. the food images and the text should be possible to edit easy. Section 3: Perfect Match is our section where we show restaurants who use our Chilioil. in the overview around 4-5 restaurants should be visible with a image, title and describtion of the restaurant and location. CTA button to find all restaurants with our chili oil (i need a seperate site of this on a later stage) Section 4: Product Overview with describtion and buy button. later there will be more options to buy like packages and other variations. maybe under this a preview of the other products? Section 5: User Ratings. (ihr seid süchtig means you're addicted). some user feedbacks which we can edit (testimonials) Section 6: Merch Shop Next to the chili oil we also sell caps and tshirt of our brand. CTA button to the shop with all products. Overall: Everything should be easy to edit (texts, images, icons). if possible we need a ZIP file of the theme at the end or is there a better option to import and work on it ? Other Sections: If you have experience you can add or edit the sections.
jobs.title: Scharf Druf Shopify Theme Design

jobs.description: I’m starting a clothing brand on BigCommerce and looking for a developer/designer to set up and customize my store. The main focus is on building a clean, modern, and professional-looking BigCommerce store using a free theme. Scope of work: * Set up and customize a BigCommerce theme (layout, colors, fonts) * Design a clean homepage (banner, featured products, branding style) * Create simple navigation (Shop, About, Contact) * Improve product page layout for a polished and trustworthy look * Ensure mobile responsiveness and overall user experience I also have designs ready on Printful that will need to be connected and synced to the store (basic integration). Budget is around $200 for this initial setup. I’m looking for someone reliable with good design sense. If the work goes well, there will be ongoing tasks and improvements. Please share: * Examples of BigCommerce or eCommerce stores you’ve worked on * Your approach to designing clothing/fashion stores Looking for someone who can deliver a clean, modern layout and understands eCommerce design basics.
jobs.title: BigCommerce Developer Needed for Clothing Brand (Theme Customization + Clean UI)

jobs.description: We are looking for a skilled Shopify Virtual Assistant to manage product listing tasks for our store. The ideal candidate will be responsible for uploading products, optimizing listings, and maintaining accurate product data to ensure a smooth shopping experience. Responsibilities: * Upload and manage product listings on Shopify * Write clear, SEO-friendly product titles and descriptions * Organize products into relevant collections * Maintain accurate pricing, images, and product details * Update and edit existing listings when required * Ensure consistency and quality across all product pages Requirements: * Proven experience with Shopify product listing * Strong attention to detail * Basic understanding of SEO for e-commerce * Ability to work efficiently and meet deadlines * Experience with product data management is a plus If you have experience managing Shopify stores and creating high-quality product listings, we’d love to hear from you. Apply now to join our team.
jobs.title: Shopify VA Product Listing Specialist
